pir: Will the tracked competitor make a material pricing change within 30 days, and if so in which direction and on which product line?

ground_truth.state: The tracked competitor, Cirrus, has internally approved a permanent reduction of its entry-level Starter plan from $49 to $39 per month, effective March 1, to blunt a cheaper challenger. The decision is approved, the sales team has been briefed, a staging pricing page already reflects the new figure, and marketing is seeding more-accessible messaging. The intensified discounting and the rumor circulating in the trade press are downstream of this real, decided change. A separate Packaging and Monetization job posting reflects a slower, parallel tier-restructuring effort and is not the pricing event itself. The decision resolves as a genuine, material price cut on the Starter line within the window.

signal_stream.content: Cirrus has run back-to-back limited-time 20 percent discounts on the Starter plan for six straight weeks, which is unusual for them and well beyond their normal promo cadence.
Cirrus posted a job opening for a Senior Product Manager, Packaging and Monetization, tasked with rethinking plan tiers and bundling.
On a widely shared podcast, the Cirrus CEO said the company wants to lower the barrier to entry and make it a no-brainer for smaller teams to get started.
A SaaS-pricing trade blog, PricingPulse, published a post claiming that sources say Cirrus is about to drop Starter pricing.
An aggregator newsletter repeated the PricingPulse claim that Cirrus will cut Starter pricing, linking back to and citing the PricingPulse post.
A growth marketer restated the same PricingPulse rumor about a Cirrus Starter price cut in a LinkedIn post, attributing it to the PricingPulse report.
Cirrus announced the opening of a new Frankfurt data-center region to meet European data-residency and compliance requirements.

A usually-reliable contact inside the Cirrus sales organization reports the team was briefed this week on new Starter-tier pricing set roughly 20 percent lower, taking effect at the start of next month.
A cached version of the Cirrus pricing page, surfaced through their CDN, shows the Starter plan at $39 per month instead of the current $49. The page is staged and not yet linked from the live site.
